programme.title: UEFA Champions League - Classics
programme.desc: We revisit the 1996/97 UEFA Champions League final between Borussia Dortmund and Juventus in Munich. Armed with an expensively assembled squad, Juve were overwhelming favourites.

programme.title: UEFA Champions League - Classics
programme.desc: Real Madrid went in search of their seventh European Cup title when they faced Juventus in the 1998 final in Amsterdam. The Serie A winners featured a 25-year-old Zinedine Zidane.

programme.title: UEFA Champions League - Classics
programme.desc: A look back at the 1999/00 UEFA Champions League final between Real Madrid and Valencia in Paris. Steve McManaman, signed from Liverpool the previous summer, featured for Madrid.

programme.title: UEFA Champions League - Classics
programme.desc: We revisit the dramatic 2001 final between Bayern Munich and Valencia in Milan. Bayern, chasing their first title in a quarter-century, featured a 20-year-old Owen Hargreaves.

programme.title: UEFA Champions League - Classics
programme.desc: The 2001/02 UEFA Champions League final saw a star-studded Real Madrid face Bayer Leverkusen in Glasgow. The match featured arguably the greatest goal in the competition's history.

programme.title: UEFA Champions League - Classics
programme.desc: We revisit the 2003/04 UEFA Champions League final between Monaco and Porto in Gelsenkirchen. Jose Mourinho would leave Porto to take over at Chelsea just days later.

programme.title: UEFA Champions League - Classics
programme.desc: We revisit the 2011/12 UEFA Champions League final between Bayern Munich and Chelsea at the Allianz Arena. This was Chelsea's second final, having lost their first in 2008.

programme.title: Live: Men's Giro d'Italia
programme.desc: Coverage from the fourth stage of the race, a 138km route from Catanzaro to Cosenza. This stage last year saw a 189km route from Alberobello to Lecce that was won by Dutch cyclist Casper van Uden, as he picked up his one and only victory of the race. Great Britain's Simon Yates won last year's race ahead of Mexico's Isaac del Toro and Ecuador's Richard Carapaz.

programme.title: UEFA Champions League - Classics

programme.title: ATP/WTA Tour Classic Matches
programme.desc: Roaring back from long-term injury, former champion Kim Clijsters would meet top seed and world number one Lindsay Davenport in the 2005 Indian Wells final.

programme.title: ATP Final: Miami
programme.desc: Chasing a record seventh men's Miami crown, Novak Djokovic - now the oldest ATP Masters 1000 finalist - takes on Jakub Mensik, aiming to win his first tour-level trophy. (30.

programme.title: WTA Final: Montreal
programme.desc: Rising Canadian star Victoria Mboko's miracle Montreal run continues as the 18-year-old meets four-time Grand Slam champion Naomi Osaka in her first tour-level final. (07.

programme.title: ATP Final: Monte Carlo
programme.desc: Chasing a sixth ATP 1000 title and a first in Monte-Carlo, 21-year-old Spaniard Carlos Alcaraz competes in a blockbuster clash with first-time Masters finalist Lorenzo Musetti. (13.

programme.title: WTA Final: Cincinnati
programme.desc: Six-time Grand Slam champion Iga Swiatek faces Jasmine Paolini for the Cincinnati title. In what will be Swiatek's 13th WTA 1000 final, Paolini is competing in just her third. (18.

programme.title: US Open Tennis Classic Matches
programme.desc: Going 10 rounds without dropping a set, Great Britain's Emma Raducanu claimed the women's title at the 2021 US Open, becoming the first-ever qualifier to win a singles Grand Slam.

programme.title: WTA Final: Madrid
programme.desc: Mirra Andreeva, entering her third WTA 1000 final at 19, competes in the Madrid Open showpiece against Marta Kostyuk, with the Ukrainian targeting the biggest title of her career. (02.

programme.title: ATP Final: Miami
programme.desc: Jannik Sinner's chance to join an exclusive list of Sunshine Double winners arrives as the Italian world number two faces the explosive Czech Jiri Lehecka in the Miami Open final. (29.

programme.title: Tennis Highlights
programme.desc: Lorenzo Musetti, a semi-finalist in the Italian capital last year, continues his bid for glory on home soil as the eighth seed takes on Francisco Cerundolo for a last 16 spot. (10.

programme.title: Tennis Highlights
